review_meta.focus: verify rev-3 blocker/warning fixes, identify new concerns from rev-3→rev-4 deltas

rev3_blockers.title: recoveryError and recoveryStartedEditorRevision not added to session-switch reset sites

evidence.helper_defined: clearedRecoveryState() helper defined at lines 357-361, returning { recoveryError: null, recoveryStartedCompositionVersion: null }
evidence.four_sites_named: createSession (line 175 in live code), archiveSession active-session branch (line 211), selectSession (line 237), forkFromMessage (line 547) — all four explicitly named at plan line 364
evidence.composable_with_clearedGuidedState: Yes — both helpers return plain object literals with no overlapping keys; spread merging is clean
evidence.nit: initialState (sessionStore.ts line 149) is constructed once at module load and used by reset() (line 777). The plan adds the helper but never names initialState as a fifth site. Without spreading ...clearedRecoveryState() into initialState, reset() will not clear recovery fields. The clearedGuidedState() helper is correctly spread into initialState at line 160; the plan must replicate this for clearedRecoveryState().

rev3_blockers.title: editorRevision increment trigger is undefined — concurrent-edit guard has no mechanically enforced semantics

evidence.rename: editorRevision renamed to recoveryStartedCompositionVersion; snapped from compositionState?.version at compose-start
evidence.compositionState_version_is_server_drive: CONFIRMED. All compositionState writes in sessionStore.ts (lines 254, 304-331, 472-498, 551-559, 600, 634, 737) originate from server responses. No client-side mutation path advances version.
evidence.local_edit_paths_outside_sessionStore: NONE FOUND. grep of all .ts/.tsx outside sessionStore shows SpecView, GraphView, SpecPanel, CommandPalette only read compositionState; no component calls set({ compositionState: ... }) directly. useHashRouter sets { activeSessionId: null }, not compositionState.
evidence.consequence: The guard fires when a second server-confirmed compose turn completes while the recovery panel is open — an unusual but real concurrency. It does NOT fire for local inspector edits because none exist. This is the correct semantic for this codebase.
evidence.wording_nit: Plan line 341 says 'edits made while a compose request is in flight trigger Apply confirmation'. This is imprecise: the guard fires when compositionState.version advances (i.e. another compose succeeds while the recovery panel is open), not on local in-flight edits. The test wording should be 'a second successful compose while the panel is open triggers confirmation'. The guard is mechanically sound; the description is misleading.
evidence.snapshot_timing: Snapshot taken at compose-start before the catch block, so the stored version is the pre-flight version. Correct: the comparison is pre-flight-version vs post-panel-open-version.

rev3_warnings.title: Integration eval characterization test not in Task 0 Step 5 focused run
rev3_warnings.evidence: tests/integration/pipeline/test_composer_llm_eval_characterization.py confirmed present at /home/john/elspeth/tests/integration/pipeline/test_composer_llm_eval_characterization.py. Added to focused run at plan line 167.

rev3_warnings.title: Fetch-once guard / audit_grade_view inflation
rev3_warnings.evidence: Task 5 line 433 adds 'fetch is called exactly once per panel open with stable dependencies'. Line 452 specifies useEffect dependency array [sessionId, failedTurn.assistant_message_id]. This bounds the counter increment to one record per panel open.

rev3_warnings.title: z-index stacking unspecified
rev3_warnings.evidence: --z-dialog confirmed at App.css:173 (value 201). Task 6 line 493 pins recovery panel to --z-dialog. Task 8 line 573 adds fanout+recovery simultaneous smoke. Fix is internally consistent with existing modal layer.

rev3_warnings.title: Retry-after-failure second panel open semantics undefined
rev3_warnings.evidence: Task 3 lines 337-338 add test cases for 'retry after discard replaces' and 'discard idempotent when no panel open'. Replacement semantics are now explicitly in the test spec.

rev3_warnings.title: applyRecoveredState server-sync contract undefined
rev3_warnings.evidence: Task 3 line 368 and Task 7 line 527 assert local-only: no PATCH, PUT, compose retry, transcript mutation, or audit-write. This is an explicit answer to the rev-3 ambiguity. CLAUDE.md auditability is satisfied: the persisted composition_states row is the audit-of-record; Apply/Discard is a UI choice on already-persisted data.

rev3_warnings.title: limit=500 pagination window misses the relevant failed-turn row
rev3_warnings.evidence: Task 5 line 440 adds test for assistant_message_id present but absent from first 500 rows, showing a degraded diagnostic. The rev-3 gap was specifically that the non-null-but-not-found case was untested.

rev3_warnings.title: No schema-version guard on partial_state deserialization
rev3_warnings.evidence: Task 1 line 233 accepts this as a documented gap. Wording is clear and scoped.

new_concerns.title: B-1 residual: initialState / reset() not named as a fifth clearedRecoveryState site
new_concerns.description: The plan threads clearedRecoveryState() through four session-switch sites (createSession, archiveSession, selectSession, forkFromMessage) but never names initialState. The clearedGuidedState() precedent (line 160 in sessionStore.ts) spreads the helper into initialState so reset() also clears those fields. Without ...clearedRecoveryState() in initialState, a hard reset (authStore calling useSessionStore.getState().reset()) will leave recoveryError and recoveryStartedCompositionVersion at their initial undefined values rather than null, which means TypeScript null-checks in the recovery panel will misbehave.
new_concerns.required_fix: Task 3 Step 2 must add ...clearedRecoveryState() to initialState alongside ...clearedGuidedState(). One line; follows the existing precedent exactly.

new_concerns.title: B-2 wording creates a misleading test expectation at line 341
new_concerns.description: Line 341 says 'edits made while a compose request is in flight trigger Apply confirmation'. compositionState.version is exclusively server-driven; there are no local-edit paths. The test case as worded cannot be satisfied literally (no mechanism to advance version without a round-trip). The correct description is 'a second successful compose turn completes while the recovery panel is open'. If an implementer takes the wording literally they will implement a local edit counter that does not exist and is not needed.
new_concerns.required_fix: Rephrase line 341 to: 'a second successful compose response arrives while the recovery panel is open, advancing compositionState.version, and then Apply is requested — this triggers confirmation'.

new_concerns.title: _failed_turn_response_body() shape forward-compatibility
new_concerns.description: The plan (Task 1 line 209-212) asserts exactly four nested fields: assistant_message_id, tool_calls_attempted, tool_responses_persisted, transcript_url. Live code at routes.py:1293-1298 emits exactly these four — confirmed. No fifth field. If a future backend adds a fifth field the TypeScript parser will silently drop it because the interface won't include it. This is an accepted structural gap in TypeScript (excess property access, not excess property error in assignments). No action required now; document as accepted when adding FailedTurn fields in Task 1 Step 2.
new_concerns.required_fix: None required now. Optional: add a comment in FailedTurn type that it mirrors routes.py:_failed_turn_response_body() to make future sync visible.

new_concerns.title: Apply/Discard audit-framing: accepted
new_concerns.description: The 'non-audited at the user-action layer' framing (Task 3 line 368) is consistent with CLAUDE.md. The persisted composition_states row is the audit-of-record per CLAUDE.md ('the Landscape audit trail is the source of truth'). Apply/Discard is a UI navigation choice on already-persisted data, not a new decision producing new Landscape state. A security reviewer wanting this audited would need a spec change to mandate it; the current spec does not. Not a systems blocker.

new_concerns.title: Integration test mock layer for 'no audit-write request sent'
new_concerns.description: Task 7 line 527 asserts 'no audit-write request is sent from Apply/Discard handlers'. The frontend test environment uses fetch mocks (vi.spyOn or MSW). Asserting 'no audit-write' requires either a catch-all handler that records all outbound fetch calls or an explicit assertion that no request matching /api/.*/audit/ is made. Standard @testing-library MSW setups will pass silently if the handler is not wired. This is an implementation detail, not a plan gap — but the test spec should explicitly name what the 'no audit-write' assertion checks (no outbound fetch to any /api/ endpoint beyond the already-mocked ones).
new_concerns.required_fix: Tighten Task 7 Step 1 wording: specify that 'no audit-write request is sent' is verified by asserting fetch was not called with any URL matching /api/ routes other than the transcript fetch already covered by Task 5 tests.

summary.critical_path: NC-1 (initialState/reset gap) must be addressed before implementation. NC-2 (wording) should be fixed to prevent implementer confusion on the concurrent-edit test. NC-3, NC-4, NC-5 are non-blocking.
